verdict: The block was RIGHT and the chart RECONSTRUCTS it: discovery grouped all three planted planes correctly (subspace overlap 0.985/0.984/0.944 with the true planes) and every K=1 chart reconstructs its ring at high EV (0.926/0.925/0.890 held-out). The one sub-0.8 circle (block 0, corr 0.771) is the STRONGEST circle (amp 2.6), so it is not a weak-signal or mis-grouping failure -- it is a chart ANGLE-FIDELITY shortfall: the n_basis=4 fourier curve parameterizes the ring slightly non-uniformly and seed selection is by reconstruction EV (which does not directly optimize angular uniformity). Fix levers (not a factorization problem): lower n_basis (nearer a pure single-winding sinusoid), a winding/arc-length regularizer, or more seeds with angular-uniformity tie-breaking. Discovery and factorization are vindicated; the residual is in the per-block chart optimizer, exactly where the nursery isolates it.
metric_note: recovered_corr = |Jammalamadaka circular correlation| of atan2(reconstruction) vs planted theta, all rows; 0.8 is an arbitrary reporting bar.
